title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: Working notes package built. part shipped package (excluded via .Rbuildignore); intended record maintainers.

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: gbtmkit generalizes one-group-based trajectory modeling (GBTM) analysis script reproducible, engine-agnostic R package follows GRoLTS reporting checklist. estimation engine pluggable (trajeR now; flexmix/lcmm designed ) behind small accessor interface, diagnostics plotting work backend, binary continuous outcomes run one specification. Repo: https://github.com/fabregithub/gbtmkit (public, MIT) Docs site: https://fabregithub.github.io/gbtmkit/ (pkgdown, auto-deployed) Author: Shoji F. Nakayama (ORCID 0000-0001-7772-0389) Full design rationale: dev/DESIGN.md

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: Name gbtmkit (confirmed free CRAN/GitHub/Bioconductor). Engine-agnostic architecture: one S3 adapter contract; trajeR first. outcome types first-class: binary (LOGIT) continuous (CNORM). Data entirely synthetic domain-neutral. real cohort data published external published dataset used, avoid revealing study domain. Two generated datasets (sim_binary, sim_continuous) known ground-truth groups ship package data drive tests, examples, vignette. License MIT. Performance/autonomy treated first-class requirement shape search (original slow ~80k rows).

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: trajeR hessian: affects standard errors. Point estimates, BIC, posterior diagnostics identical hessian = FALSE ~2.6x faster, search runs Hessian-final model refit . CNORM local optima: continuous fits fall local optima tight group separation (BIC -selects). synthetic continuous fixture uses wide separation; multi-start initialization noted future improvement. Binary low-information: 4 binary occasions cap per-subject classification (~0.83) even BIC recovers right group count; synthetic binary fixture uses wide separation recovery reliable.

what: Findings from the data redesign (2026-07: 10 occasions, mixed-degree shapes)
title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: fixtures rebuilt realistic: ten occasions, two linear groups (rising G1, falling G4) two cubic groups (peak G2, trough G3), shapes crossing mid-study. Lessons: Linear-group-number selection -selects curved data: cubic planted shapes, select_n_groups(degree = 1) picks 3 groups; degree 2 needed BIC find planted 4. Tests vignette select degree = 2. Mixed per-group degrees fragile single-start fits: fixing degrees = c(1, 3, 3, 1) (truth) pins degree arbitrary initialization slot; method “L” lands local optima (empty merged groups) fixtures. Uniform rep(3, 4) recovers cleanly, examples use per-group degrees left shape search. Binary separation must stay wide even 10 occasions: crossing trajectories put several groups similar probabilities mid-study; sep = 3 (log-odds) 4-group fit collapses. sep = 3 full pipeline recovers 4 groups (0.89 assignment recovery, entropy 0.76). trajeR’s EMIRLS fails numerically (“solve(): solution found”) 10-occasion binary fixture; select_algorithm() records moves , designed.

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: getting-started vignette runs ~20 min real fits, made every CI / pkgdown build re-run (~2 h GitHub runner). now precomputed: Source truth: vignettes/getting-started.Rmd.orig (executable, .Rbuildignored). Shipped file: vignettes/getting-started.Rmd (static: outputs baked vignettes/getting-started-*.png figures beside , committed git; builds seconds). knitr output/message hook .orig strips trajeR’s optimizer chatter; fig.path file prefix R CMD check flags vignettes/figure/ directory knitr leftover. Regenerate Rscript data-raw/precompile-vignette.R editing .orig demonstrated behavior changes, commit .orig, regenerated .Rmd, figures together. Bug-fix verification require re-knitting: tests direct runs verify code; vignette refreshed shown outputs matter. pkgdown workflow likewise builds examples = FALSE (reference examples real fits, ~50 min runner; pages show code without executed output). Since examples \donttest, nothing runs automatically anymore – run devtools::run_examples() locally release (touching roxygen example).

what: Bugs in the original script that the port fixes
title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: Duplicated, mutually inconsistent PMS filters -> single robust min-across-group summary columns apply_grolts_criteria(). test_algo[1:2] dropping third algorithm BIC comparison. --bounds test_algo[] group loop. Silently dropped failed fits (.errorhandling = "remove") -> failures now recorded NA warning. Mis-applied entropy (Shannon proportions) -> standard normalized classification entropy [0, 1].

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: Built step step, confirming stage, R CMD check kept 0/0/0 throughout.

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: Rename + git + gitignore — folder gbtm-pipeline -> gbtmkit, git init main, private data (--deleted--commit/, *.rds, *.RData) excluded history. Synthetic data — data-raw/simulate-data.R builds sim_binary (n=1500) sim_continuous (n=1200) known groups; recovery verified. Package skeleton — DESCRIPTION (MIT, engines Suggests), NAMESPACE, data docs, testthat harness; R CMD check clean. Core — (4a) gbtm_spec(), (4b) engine interface + trajeR adapter, (4c) engine-neutral diagnostics. assignment + plotting + run_gbtm_pipeline() orchestrator. Release prep — (6a) vignette + README, (6b) CI workflow + NEWS, public GitHub repo created pushed. CI — green ubuntu (release/devel/oldrel-1), macOS, Windows; CI speed/cleanup pass. Docs site — pkgdown site published GitHub Pages, auto-rebuilt every push main (deploy workflow -> gh-pages branch). design doc moved docs/ dev/DESIGN.md pkgdown can conventional docs/ output. vignette later expanded stage--stage GRoLTS walkthrough continuous-outcome plot.

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: v0.1.0 tagged (2026-07-13): three engines (trajeR / flexmix / lcmm), full GRoLTS pipeline, precomputed vignette, README scope section; CI green five platforms. R CMD check 0/0/0 locally (including pandoc-built vignette). Tests: fast logic/mock/validation tests run everywhere (~1s); 16 real-fit integration tests run locally (devtools, NOT_CRAN=true) skip CI/CRAN. Documentation site live https://fabregithub.github.io/gbtmkit/ Getting started article (one-call pipeline, stage--stage flow, binary + continuous examples) full function reference.

title: Project notes & history — gbtmkit
text: Tag v0.1.0. Second engine adapter (flexmix) prove interface generalizes — done (2026-07-12). Notes: flexmix maps GBTM mixture GLMs long data grouped subject (y ~ poly(t, d, raw = TRUE) | id); binomial needs two-column cbind(y, 1 - y) response; logLik/BIC/AIC S4 methods, accessors go stats4::; per-component degrees via FLXMRglmfix(nested=) collapse practice, adapter requires uniform degrees evaluate_shapes() sweeps uniform shapes (gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees()); flexmix::refit() (SE step) can produce NaN SEs boundary parameters — warning, error. binary fixture flexmix pipeline matches trajeR’s recovery (0.895) runs much faster. Third engine adapter (lcmm) — done (2026-07-12). Notes: GBTM = latent class growth analysis, random = ~ -1 class-specific effects via mixture =; gaussian → hlme(), binary → lcmm(link = "thresholds") (2-level ordinal ≈ probit trajectory model; conv=1 ~30 s binary fixture, recovery 0.896). ng > 1 requires starting values: fit ng = 1 first pass B (deterministic init). lcmm post-processing (predictY) re-parses stored call, adapter must patch call$fixed / call$mixture actual formula objects fitting. Model-implied group sizes = softmax first ng-1 parameters (class-membership intercepts, last class reference). mixture shared across classes, uniform degrees — capability flag flexmix. grolts_report() mapping outputs GRoLTS checklist item numbers. Multi-start initialization CNORM. Consider CRAN submission. Optional: bump JamesIves/github-pages-deploy-action clear Node 20 deprecation warning.

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: package ships two entirely synthetic datasets known ground-truth groups. sim_binary binary outcome measured ten occasions, four latent trajectory shapes mixed polynomial order: linear rising group, cubic rise-peak-decline group, cubic decline-trough-recovery group, linear falling group:

what: Describe the model with a spec
title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: gbtm_spec() records model – outcome time columns (name), id, outcome family – validates , independent engine fit .
code: spec <- gbtm_spec(   sim_binary,   outcomes = paste0("y", 1:10),   time     = paste0("t", 1:10),   id       = "id",   family   = "binomial" ) spec #> <gbtm_spec> #>   family     : binomial #>   subjects   : 1500 #>   occasions  : 10 #>   outcomes   : y1, y2, y3, y4, y5, y6, y7, y8, y9, y10 #>   time       : t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7, t8, t9, t10 #>   id         : id

what: Run the whole pipeline in one call
title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: run_gbtm_pipeline() performs algorithm selection (engine offers choice), group-number selection, polynomial-shape search GRoLTS acceptance criteria, final Hessian-fit. use small search keep vignette quick. pipeline recovers four planted groups. Everything stage produced kept result object:
code: res <- run_gbtm_pipeline(   spec,   candidates = 2:5,     # consider 2 to 5 groups   degree     = 2,       # quadratic while choosing the number of groups --                         # with curved shapes, linear-only selection under-selects   method     = "L",     # fix the algorithm (skip stage 1) for speed   max_degree = 3,       # allow up to cubic in the shape search   seed       = 1,   verbose    = FALSE ) res #> <gbtm_result> #>   engine/family : trajeR / binomial #>   method        : L #>   groups        : 4 #>   degrees       : 2, 3, 1, 3 #>   GRoLTS criteria met: TRUE #>   entropy       : 0.762  BIC: 17116.7 res$group_selection      # BIC for each candidate number of groups #> <gbtm_selection> stage=n_groups  by=BIC #>  n_groups   degrees      bic      aic   ok #>         2       2,2 17422.92 17385.72 TRUE #>         3     2,2,2 17452.17 17393.73 TRUE #>         4   2,2,2,2 17168.99 17089.29 TRUE #>         5 2,2,2,2,2 17197.93 17096.98 TRUE #>   best: 4 summary(res) #> === gbtm pipeline result === #> <gbtm_result> #>   engine/family : trajeR / binomial #>   method        : L #>   groups        : 4 #>   degrees       : 2, 3, 1, 3 #>   GRoLTS criteria met: TRUE #>   entropy       : 0.762  BIC: 17116.7 #>  #> Group diagnostics: #>  group n_assigned prop_assigned prop_model mismatch  appa    occ #>      1        325         0.217      0.197   -0.019 0.862 25.333 #>      2        290         0.193      0.217    0.023 0.902 33.189 #>      3        572         0.381      0.358   -0.023 0.891 14.634 #>      4        313         0.209      0.228    0.019 0.866 21.945 #>  #> Assigned group sizes: #>  #>   1   2   3   4  #> 325 290 572 313

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: gbtm_diagnostics() gives GRoLTS classification diagnostics, plot_trajectories() draws fitted group trajectories observed per-group means overlaid. plot chunk unnamed-chunk-8 Per-subject group assignment (analogue exporting group column) res$assignment:

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: run_gbtm_pipeline() convenience wrapper. full control can run GRoLTS stage inspect result moving – exactly wrapper internally.

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: trajeR offers "L", "EM" "EMIRLS"; one lowest BIC selected. (Engines single optimizer skip stage.)
code: algo <- select_algorithm(spec, n_groups = 2, degrees = c(1, 1)) algo #> <gbtm_selection> stage=algorithm  by=BIC #>  method       bic       aic   ok #>       L  17980.01  17953.44 TRUE #>      EM 145507.87 145481.30 TRUE #>  EMIRLS 145739.37 145712.80 TRUE #>   best: L

what: Stage 2: choose the number of groups
title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: number groups chosen BIC set candidates. Quadratic shapes used sweeping: curved trajectories like , linear-selection -selects number groups:
code: groups <- select_n_groups(spec, candidates = 2:5, degree = 2, method = "L") groups #> <gbtm_selection> stage=n_groups  by=BIC #>  n_groups   degrees      bic      aic   ok #>         2       2,2 17422.92 17385.72 TRUE #>         3     2,2,2 17452.17 17393.73 TRUE #>         4   2,2,2,2 17168.99 17089.29 TRUE #>         5 2,2,2,2,2 17197.93 17096.98 TRUE #>   best: 4

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: polynomial shapes searched chosen number groups, GRoLTS acceptance criteria (PMS > 0.05, APPA > 0.70, OCC >= 5) applied. (one-call pipeline searched cubic; cap search quadratic keep vignette quick.)
code: shapes <- evaluate_shapes(spec, n_groups = groups$best, method = "L",                           max_degree = 2, verbose = FALSE) apply_grolts_criteria(shapes) #> <gbtm_criteria> PMS>0.05, APPA>0.70, OCC>=5 | 5 shape(s) pass #>   recommended: degrees 2,2,1,1  (BIC 17157.6, entropy 0.751)

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: final model fit Hessian , fit carries standard errors, diagnostics per-subject assignment read . use lowest-BIC shape found search:

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: pipeline handles continuous outcomes: switch family "gaussian" (mapped censored-normal model) point spec continuous dataset. sim_continuous four shape types continuous scale. fit cubic shapes four groups (real analysis shape search refines per-group degrees, ). Shape misspecification can push fit degenerate local optimum group ends empty – data, forcing linear shapes (degrees = rep(1, 4)) "L" optimizer exactly . happens, plot_trajectories() / gbtm_predict() warn ; switching method ("EM" recovers four groups ) revisiting shapes fixes . plot_trajectories() works way; continuous outcome fitted lines observed points outcome’s scale (means, probabilities): plot chunk unnamed-chunk-15
code: data("sim_continuous", package = "gbtmkit") cspec <- gbtm_spec(   sim_continuous,   outcomes = paste0("y", 1:10),   time     = paste0("t", 1:10),   id       = "id",   family   = "gaussian" ) cfit <- fit_gbtm(cspec, n_groups = 4, degrees = rep(3, 4), method = "L") gbtm_diagnostics(cfit)$entropy #> [1] 1 plot_trajectories(cfit)

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: Everything used default engine, trajeR. Estimation pluggable: every fitting function (gbtm_fit(), stage functions, run_gbtm_pipeline()) takes engine argument, registry tells backend offers: three backends make different trade-offs: pipeline adapts automatically: single-optimizer engine stage 1 skipped, uniform-degree engine stage-3 shape search sweeps uniform shapes (degree 1 groups, degree 2 groups, …) instead per-group combinations. Switching engine one argument – 4-group cubic model binary data backend: three engines find four groups comparable classification quality. One caution: compare BIC within engine, never across engines – backend defines likelihood differently (lcmm’s thresholds link even fits different model family), absolute BIC values common scale. Use BIC pick number groups shape given engine; use classification diagnostics (entropy, APPA, OCC) sanity-check fit engine.
code: gbtm_engines() #> [1] "trajeR"  "flexmix" "lcmm" sapply(gbtm_engines(), gbtm_engine_families) #> $trajeR #> [1] "binomial" "gaussian" "poisson"  "beta"     #>  #> $flexmix #> [1] "binomial" "gaussian" "poisson"  #>  #> $lcmm #> [1] "binomial" "gaussian" sapply(gbtm_engines(), gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees) #>  trajeR flexmix    lcmm  #>    TRUE   FALSE   FALSE fits <- list(   trajeR  = fit_gbtm(spec, engine = "trajeR",  n_groups = 4,                      degrees = rep(3, 4), method = "L", hessian = FALSE),   flexmix = fit_gbtm(spec, engine = "flexmix", n_groups = 4,                      degrees = rep(3, 4), seed = 1),   lcmm    = fit_gbtm(spec, engine = "lcmm",    n_groups = 4,                      degrees = rep(3, 4), seed = 1) ) sapply(fits, function(f) {   d <- gbtm_diagnostics(f)   c(entropy = round(d$entropy, 3), min_appa = round(min(d$groups$appa), 3)) }) #>          trajeR flexmix  lcmm #> entropy   0.761   0.749 0.750 #> min_appa  0.851   0.846 0.844

title: Getting started with gbtmkit
text: Engine-agnostic design. pipeline talks small set accessors (gbtm_bic(), gbtm_posterior(), gbtm_group_sizes(), …), additional backends can added without changing workflow. Built scale. shape search runs fits Hessian (needed final model’s standard errors), defaults greedy stepwise strategy, supports time_budget, max_fits, -disk checkpointing large problems run unattended bounded. ```

code: @Manual{,   title = {gbtmkit: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling},   author = {Shoji F. Nakayama},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/fabregithub/gbtmkit}, }

what: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling
title: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling
text: gbtmkit turns group-based trajectory modeling (GBTM) reproducible, engine-agnostic pipeline follows GRoLTS checklist (Guidelines Reporting Latent Trajectory Studies) construction. 📖 Documentation site: https://fabregithub.github.io/gbtmkit/ — including Getting started walkthrough full function reference. One workflow, outcome. Binary (LOGIT) continuous (CNORM) outcomes run specification; count proportion families mapped . Engine-agnostic. Estimation delegated interchangeable backends (trajeR, flexmix, lcmm) behind small set accessors, GRoLTS diagnostics plotting work regardless engine. GRoLTS diagnostics built . Entropy, average posterior probability assignment (APPA), odds correct classification (OCC), group proportions computed posterior matrix. Built scale. shape search fits Hessian , defaults greedy stepwise strategy, supports time budget, fit cap, -disk checkpointing large problems run unattended bounded.

title: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling
text: estimation engines optional dependencies; install least one: Pick backend per fit engine = "trajeR" (default), "flexmix", "lcmm" gbtm_fit() / run_gbtm_pipeline().
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("fabregithub/gbtmkit") install.packages("trajeR")    # per-group polynomial degrees, L/EM/EMIRLS install.packages("flexmix")   # fast EM, one polynomial order for all groups install.packages("lcmm")      # hlme (gaussian) / thresholds link (binary)

title: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling
text: can also run stages individually: select_algorithm(), select_n_groups(), evaluate_shapes() + apply_grolts_criteria(), fit_gbtm(). See Getting started vignette full walkthrough.
code: library(gbtmkit)  data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit")  spec <- gbtm_spec(   sim_binary,   outcomes = paste0("y", 1:10),   time     = paste0("t", 1:10),   id       = "id",   family   = "binomial" )  # Algorithm + group-number selection, shape search with GRoLTS criteria, # and the final Hessian-on fit -- in one call. res <- run_gbtm_pipeline(spec, candidates = 2:6)  summary(res) plot_trajectories(res$final_fit) head(res$assignment)          # per-subject group membership

what: What it does — and does not do
title: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling
text: scope. Nagin-style GBTM (latent class growth analysis): latent group follows polynomial trajectory time, within-group random effects. top one model class, gbtmkit standardizes whole GRoLTS workflow: optimizer selection (engines offer choice), group-number selection BIC, bounded polynomial-shape search GRoLTS acceptance criteria (PMS, APPA, OCC), final fit standard errors, per-subject group assignment. Binary continuous outcomes work three engines; counts (Poisson) trajeR/flexmix; proportions (beta) trajeR . scope (currently). covariates. Trajectories functions time ; covariates outcome class membership yet exposed (x1/x2 columns demo data deliberately inert). growth mixture models. within-class random effects; need GMM, use lcmm::hlme() random = formula (similar tools) directly. Per-group polynomial degrees trajeR-. flexmix lcmm fit one polynomial order shared groups; shape search adapts automatically (see gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees()). cross-engine BIC. Engines define likelihoods differently, compare BIC within engine; use classification diagnostics (entropy, APPA, OCC) sanity-check fits across engines. zero-inflated counts, dual/multi-trajectory models, distal outcomes, joint survival models.

title: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling
text: example datasets sim_binary sim_continuous entirely synthetic, generated data-raw/simulate-data.R known ground-truth group membership pipeline’s recovery can verified. real data included.

title: Engine-Agnostic Pipeline for Group-Based Trajectory Modeling
text: MIT © Shoji F. Nakayama

what: Apply GRoLTS acceptance criteria to a shape table — apply_grolts_criteria
title: Apply GRoLTS acceptance criteria to a shape table — apply_grolts_criteria
text: Keeps candidate shapes whose worst group satisfies GRoLTS adequacy thresholds orders survivors chosen criterion. recommended model top row.

title: Apply GRoLTS acceptance criteria to a shape table — apply_grolts_criteria
code: apply_grolts_criteria(   shapes,   pms_min = 0.05,   appa_min = 0.7,   occ_min = 5,   order_by = "bic" )

title: Apply GRoLTS acceptance criteria to a shape table — apply_grolts_criteria
text: shapes gbtm_shapes object $table data frame (evaluate_shapes()). pms_min Minimum smallest-group assigned proportion (default 0.05). appa_min Minimum average posterior probability assignment (default 0.70). occ_min Minimum odds correct classification (default 5). order_by Column sort survivors , ascending (default "bic").

title: Apply GRoLTS acceptance criteria to a shape table — apply_grolts_criteria
text: data frame surviving shapes ordered order_by, carrying attribute "recommended" (first row, NULL none qualify) "thresholds". Class gbtm_criteria.

title: Apply GRoLTS acceptance criteria to a shape table — apply_grolts_criteria
code: # \donttest{ data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),                   paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial") if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE)) {   sh <- evaluate_shapes(spec, n_groups = 4, method = "L", verbose = FALSE)   apply_grolts_criteria(sh) } # }

what: Stage 3: search polynomial shapes for a fixed number of groups — evaluate_shapes
title: Stage 3: search polynomial shapes for a fixed number of groups — evaluate_shapes
text: Fits candidate per-group polynomial degrees records GRoLTS diagnostics , returning one row per evaluated shape. search runs unattended bounded: greedy "stepwise" strategy default, optional hard time_budget/max_fits, -disk checkpointing resume, -front run-time estimate.

title: Stage 3: search polynomial shapes for a fixed number of groups — evaluate_shapes
code: evaluate_shapes(   spec,   n_groups,   engine = gbtm_engines(),   method = NULL,   strategy = c("stepwise", "grid"),   min_degree = 1L,   max_degree = 3L,   max_passes = 2L,   hessian = FALSE,   itermax = 100L,   seed = NULL,   time_budget = Inf,   max_fits = Inf,   checkpoint = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Stage 3: search polynomial shapes for a fixed number of groups — evaluate_shapes
text: spec gbtm_spec. n_groups Number groups. engine Engine name; see gbtm_engines(). method Estimation method (e.g. winner select_algorithm()). strategy "stepwise" (default, greedy coordinate descent) "grid" (full Cartesian product degrees). min_degree, max_degree Per-group polynomial degree bounds. max_passes Maximum coordinate-descent passes "stepwise". hessian Logical; keep FALSE search (default). itermax, seed Passed gbtm_fit(). time_budget Wall-clock limit: seconds, string like "2h". Inf limit. max_fits Maximum number model fits. Inf limit. checkpoint Optional file path; results appended rerun resumes, skipping shapes already evaluated. verbose Print run-time estimate progress messages.

title: Stage 3: search polynomial shapes for a fixed number of groups — evaluate_shapes
text: object class gbtm_shapes: $table (one row per shape), $best (degrees lowest BIC), $best_fit, $n_fits, $budget_hit, $strategy.

what: Fit the final chosen trajectory model — fit_gbtm
title: Fit the final chosen trajectory model — fit_gbtm
text: thin wrapper gbtm_fit() intended final model, number groups polynomial shape chosen. differs defaulting hessian = TRUE, result includes standard errors.

title: Fit the final chosen trajectory model — fit_gbtm
code: fit_gbtm(   spec,   n_groups,   degrees,   method = NULL,   engine = gbtm_engines(),   hessian = TRUE,   itermax = 100L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Fit the final chosen trajectory model — fit_gbtm
text: spec gbtm_spec. n_groups Number latent groups. degrees Integer vector polynomial degrees, length n_groups. method Estimation method; must one gbtm_engine_methods() chosen engine (ignored engines single optimizer). engine Engine name; see gbtm_engines(). hessian Logical; compute Hessian (standard errors). Default TRUE. itermax Maximum optimizer iterations. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. ... Passed underlying engine call.

title: Fit the final chosen trajectory model — fit_gbtm
text: gbtm_fit object.

title: Fit the final chosen trajectory model — fit_gbtm
code: # \donttest{ data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),                   paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial") if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE))   fit_gbtm(spec, n_groups = 4, degrees = rep(3, 4), method = "L") # }

what: Engine-agnostic accessors for a fitted model — gbtm_accessors
title: Engine-agnostic accessors for a fitted model — gbtm_accessors
text: Read quantities pipeline needs gbtm_fit without knowing engine produced .

title: Engine-agnostic accessors for a fitted model — gbtm_accessors
code: gbtm_bic(fit, ...)  gbtm_aic(fit, ...)  gbtm_loglik(fit, ...)  gbtm_posterior(fit, ...)  gbtm_group_sizes(fit, ...)  gbtm_n_groups(fit, ...)  gbtm_degrees(fit, ...)

title: Engine-agnostic accessors for a fitted model — gbtm_accessors
text: fit gbtm_fit object. ... Unused.

title: Engine-agnostic accessors for a fitted model — gbtm_accessors
text: gbtm_bic(), gbtm_aic(), gbtm_loglik(): single numeric. gbtm_posterior(): subjects x groups matrix posterior probabilities (rows sum 1). gbtm_group_sizes(): length-n_groups vector model-implied group proportions (sums 1). gbtm_n_groups(): integer number groups. gbtm_degrees(): integer vector polynomial degrees.

what: Hard group assignment for a fitted model — gbtm_assign
title: Hard group assignment for a fitted model — gbtm_assign
text: Assigns subject group highest posterior probability.

title: Hard group assignment for a fitted model — gbtm_assign
code: gbtm_assign(fit)

title: Hard group assignment for a fitted model — gbtm_assign
text: fit gbtm_fit object.

title: Hard group assignment for a fitted model — gbtm_assign
text: data frame id, assigned group, one posterior probability column per group.

title: Hard group assignment for a fitted model — gbtm_assign
code: data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") # (fit a model first, then:)  gbtm_assign(fit)

what: GRoLTS classification diagnostics for a fitted model — gbtm_diagnostics
title: GRoLTS classification diagnostics for a fitted model — gbtm_diagnostics
text: Computes group-based trajectory fit diagnostics used GRoLTS checklist – assigned model-implied group proportions mismatch, average posterior probability assignment (APPA), odds correct classification (OCC), normalized classification entropy – entirely posterior matrix model group sizes, result identical regardless estimation engine.

title: GRoLTS classification diagnostics for a fitted model — gbtm_diagnostics
code: gbtm_diagnostics(fit, ...)

title: GRoLTS classification diagnostics for a fitted model — gbtm_diagnostics
text: fit gbtm_fit object. ... Unused.

title: GRoLTS classification diagnostics for a fitted model — gbtm_diagnostics
text: object class gbtm_diagnostics: list groups (per-group data frame), scalar entropy, n, n_groups. called fit also carries bic, aic, loglik, degrees.

title: GRoLTS classification diagnostics for a fitted model — gbtm_diagnostics
code: # \donttest{ data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),                   paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial") if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE)) {   fit <- gbtm_fit(spec, n_groups = 4, degrees = rep(3, 4), seed = 1)   gbtm_diagnostics(fit) } # }

what: Does an engine support per-group polynomial degrees? — gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees
title: Does an engine support per-group polynomial degrees? — gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees
text: trajeR fits separate polynomial order per group. flexmix lcmm fit one model formula shared components/classes, degree uniform across groups; evaluate_shapes() sweeps uniform shapes instead per-group combinations.

title: Does an engine support per-group polynomial degrees? — gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees
code: gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees(engine = gbtm_engines())

title: Does an engine support per-group polynomial degrees? — gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees
text: engine Engine name; see gbtm_engines().

title: Does an engine support per-group polynomial degrees? — gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees
text: TRUE degrees may differ across groups, FALSE otherwise.

title: Registered estimation engines — gbtm_engines
text: Registered estimation engines

code: gbtm_engines()

title: Registered estimation engines — gbtm_engines
text: Character vector engine names usable engine argument gbtm_fit().

title: Supported outcome families — gbtm_families
text: neutral family names understood gbtm_spec(). engine adapter maps idiom (e.g. trajeR maps "binomial" LOGIT, "gaussian" CNORM).

title: Supported outcome families — gbtm_families
code: gbtm_families()

title: Supported outcome families — gbtm_families

what: Fit a group-based trajectory model — gbtm_fit
title: Fit a group-based trajectory model — gbtm_fit
text: Dispatches adapter engine, returning gbtm_fit object rest pipeline reads engine-agnostic accessors (gbtm_bic(), gbtm_posterior(), gbtm_group_sizes(), ...).

title: Fit a group-based trajectory model — gbtm_fit
code: gbtm_fit(   spec,   engine = gbtm_engines(),   n_groups,   degrees,   method = NULL,   hessian = FALSE,   itermax = 100L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Fit a group-based trajectory model — gbtm_fit
text: spec gbtm_spec. engine Engine name; see gbtm_engines(). n_groups Number latent groups. degrees Integer vector polynomial degrees, length n_groups. method Estimation method; must one gbtm_engine_methods() chosen engine (ignored engines single optimizer). hessian Logical; compute Hessian (standard errors). Default FALSE speed model search – set TRUE final model. itermax Maximum optimizer iterations. seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. ... Passed underlying engine call.

title: Fit a group-based trajectory model — gbtm_fit
text: gbtm_fit object (subclassed per engine).

what: Fitted group trajectories over time — gbtm_predict
title: Fitted group trajectories over time — gbtm_predict
text: Returns group's fitted trajectory outcome scale (probability binomial, mean gaussian, rate poisson) grid times, computed model coefficients – engine-neutral, drives plot_trajectories() backend.

title: Fitted group trajectories over time — gbtm_predict
code: gbtm_predict(fit, times = NULL, n = 100L, ...)

title: Fitted group trajectories over time — gbtm_predict
text: fit gbtm_fit object. times Optional numeric vector times; defaults grid spanning observed range. n Number grid points times NULL. ... Unused.

title: Fitted group trajectories over time — gbtm_predict
text: data frame columns group, time, fitted.

what: Create a group-based trajectory model specification — gbtm_spec
title: Create a group-based trajectory model specification — gbtm_spec
text: Builds validated gbtm_spec object describing data model fit, without committing estimation engine. Columns selected name, outcome values checked declared family.

title: Create a group-based trajectory model specification — gbtm_spec
code: gbtm_spec(   data,   outcomes,   time,   id = NULL,   family = gbtm_families(),   covariates = NULL,   ymin = NULL,   ymax = NULL,   ssigma = FALSE )

title: Create a group-based trajectory model specification — gbtm_spec
text: data data frame (matrix column names) wide format: one row per subject, one column per outcome occasion. outcomes Character vector outcome column names, time order. time Character vector time/occasion column names, length outcomes. id Optional name subject-identifier column. NULL, row numbers used. supplied must contain duplicates. family Outcome family; one gbtm_families(). "binomial" binary outcomes (LOGIT), "gaussian" continuous (CNORM), "poisson" counts, "beta" proportions. covariates Optional character vector covariate column names (reserved group-membership models; used basic pipeline). ymin, ymax Optional censoring bounds continuous ("gaussian") outcomes; passed engines support censored-normal models. ssigma Logical; continuous outcomes, whether residual variance shared across groups. Default FALSE.

title: Create a group-based trajectory model specification — gbtm_spec
text: object class gbtm_spec: list validated data, outcomes, time, id, family, covariates, options.

title: Create a group-based trajectory model specification — gbtm_spec
code: data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(   sim_binary,   outcomes = paste0("y", 1:10),   time     = paste0("t", 1:10),   id       = "id",   family   = "binomial" ) spec

what: gbtmkit: Engine-Agnostic Group-Based Trajectory Modeling — gbtmkit-package
title: gbtmkit: Engine-Agnostic Group-Based Trajectory Modeling — gbtmkit-package
text: generic, reproducible pipeline group-based trajectory modeling (GBTM) follows GRoLTS reporting checklist. Estimation delegated interchangeable backends behind single interface, binary continuous outcomes handled one specification.

title: gbtmkit: Engine-Agnostic Group-Based Trajectory Modeling — gbtmkit-package
text: See design document (dev/DESIGN.md) architecture, getting-started vignette worked example synthetic datasets sim_binary sim_continuous.

title: gbtmkit: Engine-Agnostic Group-Based Trajectory Modeling — gbtmkit-package
text: Maintainer: Shoji F. Nakayama nies.sfn@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Shoji F. Nakayama nies.sfn@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: Plot fitted group trajectories — plot_trajectories
text: Draws group's fitted trajectory time , optionally, overlays observed mean outcome subjects assigned group occasion. Requires ggplot2 package.

title: Plot fitted group trajectories — plot_trajectories
code: plot_trajectories(fit, observed = TRUE, n = 100L)

title: Plot fitted group trajectories — plot_trajectories
text: fit gbtm_fit object. observed Logical; overlay observed per-group occasion means (default TRUE). n Number time grid points fitted lines.

title: Plot fitted group trajectories — plot_trajectories
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot fitted group trajectories — plot_trajectories
code: # \donttest{ data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),                   paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial") if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   fit <- fit_gbtm(spec, n_groups = 4, degrees = rep(3, 4), method = "L")   plot_trajectories(fit) } # }

what: Run the full group-based trajectory pipeline — run_gbtm_pipeline
title: Run the full group-based trajectory pipeline — run_gbtm_pipeline
text: Executes algorithm selection (optional), group-number selection, polynomial shape search GRoLTS acceptance criteria, final Hessian-fit, returning intermediate results one object.

title: Run the full group-based trajectory pipeline — run_gbtm_pipeline
code: run_gbtm_pipeline(   spec,   engine = gbtm_engines(),   candidates = 2:6,   degree = 3L,   method = NULL,   algo_n_groups = NULL,   algo_degree = NULL,   strategy = c("stepwise", "grid"),   min_degree = 1L,   max_degree = 3L,   max_passes = 2L,   pms_min = 0.05,   appa_min = 0.7,   occ_min = 5,   itermax = 100L,   seed = NULL,   time_budget = Inf,   max_fits = Inf,   checkpoint = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Run the full group-based trajectory pipeline — run_gbtm_pipeline
text: spec gbtm_spec. engine Engine name; see gbtm_engines(). candidates Integer vector group numbers consider (stage 2). degree Polynomial degree used group-number selection. method Estimation method. NULL engine offers choice, stage 1 selects ; otherwise method used throughout. algo_n_groups, algo_degree Group count degree used stage-1 algorithm selection (defaults: max(candidates), degree). strategy, min_degree, max_degree, max_passes Shape-search controls, passed evaluate_shapes(). pms_min, appa_min, occ_min GRoLTS thresholds, passed apply_grolts_criteria(). itermax, seed Passed fitting stages. time_budget, max_fits, checkpoint Bounds shape search. verbose Print progress messages. ... Passed underlying fitting calls.

title: Run the full group-based trajectory pipeline — run_gbtm_pipeline
text: object class gbtm_result elements spec, engine, method, algorithm_selection, group_selection, n_groups, shapes, criteria, chosen_degrees, criteria_met, final_fit, assignment, diagnostics, call.

title: Run the full group-based trajectory pipeline — run_gbtm_pipeline
text: shape meets GRoLTS criteria, pipeline falls back lowest-BIC shape records criteria_met = FALSE.

title: Run the full group-based trajectory pipeline — run_gbtm_pipeline
code: # \donttest{ data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),                   paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial") if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE)) {   res <- run_gbtm_pipeline(spec, candidates = 2:5, method = "L")   res } # }

what: Stage 1: select the estimation algorithm — select_algorithm
title: Stage 1: select the estimation algorithm — select_algorithm
text: Fits fixed group number shape candidate estimation method picks one lowest BIC. engines single optimizer (see gbtm_engine_methods()) -op returns method.

title: Stage 1: select the estimation algorithm — select_algorithm
code: select_algorithm(   spec,   engine = gbtm_engines(),   n_groups,   degrees,   methods = NULL,   by = c("bic", "aic"),   hessian = FALSE,   itermax = 100L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Stage 1: select the estimation algorithm — select_algorithm
text: spec gbtm_spec. engine Engine name; see gbtm_engines(). n_groups Number groups use comparison. degrees Integer vector polynomial degrees, length n_groups. methods Character vector methods compare; defaults methods engine offers. Criterion minimize, "bic" (default) "aic". hessian, itermax, seed, ... Passed gbtm_fit(). hessian defaults FALSE (selection need standard errors).

title: Stage 1: select the estimation algorithm — select_algorithm
text: gbtm_selection object $table, $best (chosen method), fitted models.

title: Stage 1: select the estimation algorithm — select_algorithm
code: # \donttest{ data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),                   paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial") if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE))   select_algorithm(spec, n_groups = 4, degrees = rep(1, 4)) # }

what: Stage 2: select the number of groups — select_n_groups
title: Stage 2: select the number of groups — select_n_groups
text: Fits model candidate number groups picks one lowest BIC. candidate uses polynomial degree degree every group unless per-candidate degrees list supplied.

title: Stage 2: select the number of groups — select_n_groups
code: select_n_groups(   spec,   engine = gbtm_engines(),   candidates = 2:6,   degree = 3L,   degrees = NULL,   method = NULL,   by = c("bic", "aic"),   hessian = FALSE,   itermax = 100L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Stage 2: select the number of groups — select_n_groups
text: spec gbtm_spec. engine Engine name; see gbtm_engines(). candidates Integer vector group numbers try (default 2:6). degree Single polynomial degree applied every group (default 3, cubic). Ignored degrees supplied. degrees Optional list, one integer vector per candidate, full control shape group number. method Estimation method use (e.g. winner select_algorithm()). Criterion minimize, "bic" (default) "aic". hessian, itermax, seed, ... Passed gbtm_fit().

title: Stage 2: select the number of groups — select_n_groups
text: gbtm_selection object $table, $best (chosen number groups), fitted models.

title: Stage 2: select the number of groups — select_n_groups
code: # \donttest{ data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit") spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),                   paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial") if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE))   select_n_groups(spec, candidates = 2:5, degree = 2) # }

title: Synthetic binary trajectory data — sim_binary
text: simulated dataset four planted latent groups, demonstrating testing binary (LOGIT / family = "binomial") group-based trajectory models. entirely synthetic domain-neutral; true group labels included recovery can checked.

title: Synthetic binary trajectory data — sim_binary
code: sim_binary

title: Synthetic binary trajectory data — sim_binary
text: data frame 1500 rows 24 columns: id Subject identifier. x1, x2 Covariates, present driving outcome. y1, y2, y3, y4, y5, y6, y7, y8, y9, y10 Binary outcomes occasions 1–10. t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7, t8, t9, t10 Occasion times. true_group Ground-truth group label (1–4); used fitting.

title: Synthetic binary trajectory data — sim_binary
text: four groups follow distinct log-odds trajectories ten occasions, mixing polynomial orders: linear rising (group 1), cubic rise-peak-decline (group 2), cubic decline-trough-recovery (group 3), linear falling (group 4). Generated data-raw/simulate-data.R fixed seed.

title: Synthetic continuous trajectory data — sim_continuous
text: simulated dataset four planted latent groups, demonstrating testing continuous (CNORM / family = "gaussian") group-based trajectory models. Entirely synthetic domain-neutral, true group labels included recovery can checked.

title: Synthetic continuous trajectory data — sim_continuous
code: sim_continuous

title: Synthetic continuous trajectory data — sim_continuous
text: data frame 1200 rows 24 columns: id Subject identifier. x1, x2 Covariates, present driving outcome. y1, y2, y3, y4, y5, y6, y7, y8, y9, y10 Continuous outcomes occasions 1–10. t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6, t7, t8, t9, t10 Occasion times. true_group Ground-truth group label (1–4); used fitting.

title: Synthetic continuous trajectory data — sim_continuous
text: four groups follow distinct mean trajectories ten occasions, mixing polynomial orders: linear rising (group 1), cubic rise-peak-decline (group 2), cubic decline-trough-recovery (group 3), linear falling (group 4), Gaussian within-group noise. Separation generous relative noise single-start CNORM fit avoids local optima. Generated data-raw/simulate-data.R fixed seed.

title: gbtmkit 0.1.0
text: Third estimation engine: lcmm (engine = "lcmm"). Gaussian outcomes map lcmm::hlme() binary outcomes lcmm::lcmm(link = "thresholds"), latent class growth models (random = ~ -1) canonical 1-class-fit initialization. Single optimizer; uniform polynomial order across groups (like flexmix). Second estimation engine: flexmix (engine = "flexmix"), proving adapter interface generalizes. Supports binomial, gaussian, poisson families single EM optimizer; polynomial order shared groups (gbtm_engine_per_group_degrees()), evaluate_shapes() sweeps uniform shapes engines. First working version: engine-agnostic pipeline group-based trajectory modeling (GBTM) follows GRoLTS reporting checklist. gbtm_spec() describes data model (columns name, outcome family) validation. engine interface (gbtm_fit() accessors) trajeR adapter supporting binary (LOGIT) continuous (CNORM) outcomes; design allows backends without changing pipeline. Engine-neutral GRoLTS diagnostics: entropy, APPA, OCC, group proportions mismatch (gbtm_diagnostics()), plus gbtm_assign(). Pipeline stages: select_algorithm(), select_n_groups(), evaluate_shapes() (bounded, checkpointing shape search) apply_grolts_criteria(), fit_gbtm(). run_gbtm_pipeline() runs whole workflow one call returns gbtm_result; plot_trajectories() draws fitted trajectories. Two synthetic example datasets, sim_binary sim_continuous: ten occasions, four planted groups mixed polynomial order (two linear, two cubic).
